title: Chewy Turkish Mushroom Tart
description: A festive Turkish tart packed with umami mushrooms, aromatic herbs, and a chewy puff pastry crust, perfect for a kosher-friendly celebration.
instructions: Roll out the puff pastry sheet on a lightly floured surface to fit a 9‑inch tart pan. Gently transfer the dough into the pan, pressing it into the corners and trimming any excess.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium‑high heat. Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they start to brown and release liquid, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the skillet. Cook, stirring frequently, until the onion is translucent and the mixture is fragrant, about 3 minutes.

Stir in the remaining tablespoon of olive oil, ground sumac, dried oregano, salt, pepper, chopped parsley, and dill. Cook for an additional 2 minutes, then remove the skillet from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly.
Spread the mushroom and herb mixture evenly over the puff pastry base.
If using an egg wash, whisk the egg and brush the exposed edges of the pastry lightly to promote a golden crust.
Place the tart in the preheated oven and bake for 20‑25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is bubbling.
Remove the tart from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serve warm, optionally garnished with extra fresh dill or a squeeze of lemon juice for brightness.
